pattern: Every split into good subarrays is completely determined by where the cut falls between each pair of consecutive 1s, so the whole count collapses to a product of gap sizes with no search at all.
hints: A good subarray has exactly one 1 in it, so once you know the positions of every 1 in the array, each split is really just a choice of one cut point strictly between each adjacent pair of 1s.
Between two consecutive 1s at positions p and q, the cut can land in any of q - p spots without changing which 1 ends up in which piece -- and that choice is independent of every other gap's choice.
Multiply the gap sizes between consecutive 1-positions together, taking the result modulo 1e9+7; if the array has no 1s at all, there is no way to form even a single good subarray.

edge_inputs.description: single 1 surrounded by zeros, only one way to split
two adjacent 1s, only one way to split
alternating so every gap is exactly 1
one very wide gap dominates the product
1s only at the very ends, large gap between them

stress.description: 100000-length random binary array, guaranteed at least one 1
